---
title: "Resume"
body-classes: text-page cv-page
---

::: page-intro
Condensed version of the formal record. The fuller context for the work itself lives on the [research](research.qmd) page.
:::

::: page-shell
```{=html}
<div class="resume-header">
  <p class="resume-name">Emily Miller</p>
  <p class="resume-tagline">Environmental data scientist working across remote sensing, geospatial analysis, and reproducible research workflows.</p>
  <div class="resume-contact">
    <a href="mailto:ermiller@ucsb.edu">ermiller@ucsb.edu</a>
    <span>(978) 265-9348</span>
    <span>Santa Barbara, CA</span>
    <a href="https://rellimylime.github.io">rellimylime.github.io</a>
    <a href="https://github.com/rellimylime">GitHub</a>
    <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/emily-miller-a0237233b/">LinkedIn</a>
  </div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<details class="page-foldout resume-context-foldout">
  <summary>Current project context</summary>
  <div class="page-foldout-body">
    <p>Current work is spread across the <a href="https://www.waveslab.org/">WaVeS Lab</a>, the <a href="https://www.landscapesofchangelab.com/">LOC Lab</a>, and a MEDS capstone with the <a href="https://www.nceas.ucsb.edu/wildfire-resilience-index">Wildfire Resilience Index</a> team at NCEAS. The project-level narratives live on the <a href="research.qmd">research</a> page.</p>
  </div>
</details>
```

## Education

```{=html}
<section class="resume-section">
  <article class="resume-entry">
    <div class="resume-entry-head">
      <div>
        <h3 class="resume-entry-title">Master of Environmental Data Science</h3>
        <p class="resume-entry-org">Bren School of Environmental Science &amp; Management, University of California, Santa Barbara</p>
      </div>
      <p class="resume-date">Expected June 2026</p>
    </div>
    <p class="resume-detail"><strong>GPA:</strong> 4.00</p>
    <p class="resume-detail"><strong>Highlighted coursework:</strong> Machine Learning in Environmental Science, Geospatial Analysis and Remote Sensing, Environmental Systems Modeling, Environmental Datasets and Data Engineering, Databases and Data Management, Statistics for Environmental Data Science</p>
    <p class="resume-detail"><strong>Leadership:</strong> MEDS Student Faculty Representative</p>
  </article>

  <article class="resume-entry">
    <div class="resume-entry-head">
      <div>
        <h3 class="resume-entry-title">Bachelor of Science in Mathematics</h3>
        <p class="resume-entry-org">University of California, Santa Barbara</p>
      </div>
      <p class="resume-date">June 2025</p>
    </div>
    <p class="resume-detail"><strong>GPA:</strong> 3.67</p>
    <p class="resume-detail"><strong>Highlighted coursework:</strong> Real Analysis, Linear Algebra, Numerical Analysis, Computer Science and Problem Solving, Probability and Statistics</p>
    <p class="resume-detail"><strong>Leadership:</strong> Undergraduate Outreach Officer for Women in Science and Engineering (WiSE)</p>
  </article>
</section>
```

## Research and Data Analysis Experience

```{=html}
<section class="resume-section">
  <article class="resume-entry">
    <div class="resume-entry-head">
      <div>
        <h3 class="resume-entry-title">Research Data Analyst</h3>
        <p class="resume-entry-org">Landscapes of Change (LOC) Lab, Bren School of Environmental Science &amp; Management, UCSB</p>
      </div>
      <p class="resume-date">January 2026 - Present</p>
    </div>
    <ul>
      <li>Built reproducible data pipelines in R and Python integrating five federal datasets including USDA Forest Service aerial surveys, TerraClimate, PRISM, WorldClim, and FIA into analysis-ready formats using centralized YAML configuration and <code>renv</code>.</li>
      <li>Developed pixel decomposition architecture to spatially link forest disturbance observations to gridded climate data via area-weighted joins, extracting pixel-level values through Google Earth Engine with roughly 300 GB of processed output.</li>
      <li>Produced plot-level forest inventory summaries including tree metrics, diversity indices, disturbance history, and site-level climate records from 1958 to present for thermophilization analysis across roughly 7,000 FIA sites.</li>
    </ul>
  </article>

  <article class="resume-entry">
    <div class="resume-entry-head">
      <div>
        <h3 class="resume-entry-title">Research Assistant</h3>
        <p class="resume-entry-org">Water Vegetation and Society (WaVeS) Lab, University of California, Santa Barbara</p>
      </div>
      <p class="resume-date">June 2024 - Present</p>
    </div>
    <ul>
      <li>Applied statistical and numerical methods to analyze and validate agricultural water and land-use datasets across multiple spatial scales.</li>
      <li>Built Python pipelines using Rasterio, GeoPandas, and Shapely to process multiband raster and vector datasets, applying CRS transformations, spatial joins, raster clipping, and reprojection across heterogeneous sources at continental scale.</li>
      <li>Created statistical frameworks and visualization methods for examining complex spatial relationships in agricultural systems.</li>
    </ul>
  </article>

  <article class="resume-entry">
    <div class="resume-entry-head">
      <div>
        <h3 class="resume-entry-title">BEL Fellow</h3>
        <p class="resume-entry-org">Bren Environmental Leadership Program, University of California, Santa Barbara</p>
      </div>
      <p class="resume-date">June 2024 - December 2024</p>
    </div>
    <ul>
      <li>Built a multi-source R and Python pipeline integrating five federal forest datasets across 4.47 million records and roughly 300 GB of output.</li>
      <li>Extracted pixel-level climate data at IDS polygon locations via Google Earth Engine using a pixel decomposition approach with area-weighted coverage fractions across all climate datasets.</li>
      <li>Engineered reproducible workflows with <code>renv</code>, config-driven architecture, and a Streamlit dashboard for data exploration and schema documentation.</li>
    </ul>
  </article>
</section>
```

## Leadership and Community Engagement

```{=html}
<section class="resume-section">
  <article class="resume-entry">
    <div class="resume-entry-head">
      <div>
        <h3 class="resume-entry-title">Undergraduate Outreach Officer</h3>
        <p class="resume-entry-org">Women in Science and Engineering (WiSE), University of California, Santa Barbara</p>
      </div>
      <p class="resume-date">September 2024 - June 2025</p>
    </div>
    <ul>
      <li>Coordinated a mentorship program supporting more than 130 undergraduate students pursuing STEM degrees.</li>
      <li>Focused outreach efforts on increasing diversity and representation in quantitative sciences through targeted programming.</li>
      <li>Developed and implemented engagement strategies to support retention of underrepresented students in STEM fields.</li>
    </ul>
  </article>

  <article class="resume-entry">
    <div class="resume-entry-head">
      <div>
        <h3 class="resume-entry-title">Founder and Lead Organizer</h3>
        <p class="resume-entry-org">IV Recovery Community Initiative, Goleta</p>
      </div>
      <p class="resume-date">2023 - Present</p>
    </div>
    <ul>
      <li>Founded and led a bi-weekly coastal cleanup initiative engaging university students and community members.</li>
      <li>Developed community engagement strategies to increase environmental stewardship and normalize university participation in local conservation.</li>
    </ul>
  </article>
</section>
```

## Teaching Experience

```{=html}
<section class="resume-section">
  <article class="resume-entry">
    <div class="resume-entry-head">
      <div>
        <h3 class="resume-entry-title">Mathematics Tutor</h3>
        <p class="resume-entry-org">Davis, California</p>
      </div>
      <p class="resume-date">2020 - 2023</p>
    </div>
    <ul>
      <li>Taught mathematics from elementary through college level, including comprehensive GED preparation, adapting instruction to diverse learning needs and resulting in student success on examinations.</li>
      <li>Developed individualized learning plans to accommodate different learning styles and optimize student comprehension.</li>
    </ul>
  </article>
</section>
```

## Presentations

```{=html}
<section class="resume-section">
  <div class="resume-entry resume-list-entry">
    <ul class="resume-presentation-list">
      <li>Miller E, Boser A, Caylor K. "Satellite Data Reveal Emerging Decentralized Irrigation Systems in Sub-Saharan Africa." <em>Summer@Bren 2024 Flash Talks</em>, UCSB, CA. August 29, 2024.</li>
      <li>Miller E, Boser A, Caylor K. "Water Source Attribution for Center Pivot Irrigation in Sub-Saharan Africa." <em>Mantell Symposium in Environmental Justice and Conservation Innovation</em>, UCSB, CA. October 24, 2024.</li>
      <li>Boser A, Miller E, Perez J, Caylor K. "Analyzing the sustainability and climate resilience of rapidly expanding center pivot irrigation in Sub-Saharan Africa using remote sensing." <em>American Geophysical Union</em>, Washington, D.C. December 9-13, 2024.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>
</section>
```

## Skills

```{=html}
<section class="resume-section resume-skills-grid">
  <article class="resume-skill-group">
    <h3>Programming and Data Analysis</h3>
    <p>Python (Rasterio, GeoPandas, Shapely, scikit-learn, NumPy), R (tidyverse, <code>renv</code>, terra), SQL/PostgreSQL, C++</p>
  </article>

  <article class="resume-skill-group">
    <h3>Technical Skills</h3>
    <p>Statistical analysis, numerical methods, multiband raster processing, spatial joins and CRS transformations, Google Earth Engine, remote sensing, data visualization, reproducible pipeline development</p>
  </article>

  <article class="resume-skill-group">
    <h3>Mathematical Expertise</h3>
    <p>Real analysis, linear algebra, probability and statistics, numerical analysis, matrix operations, eigenvalue calculations</p>
  </article>

  <article class="resume-skill-group">
    <h3>Software and Tools</h3>
    <p>GitHub Actions, AWS S3, Streamlit, config-driven reproducible workflows</p>
  </article>
</section>
```
:::
